frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre beta, bura mat maanna, par aajkal zamaana theek nahi hai. Ladki ko khud hi sambhal ke rehna padta hai—raat ko der se aana, akeli ghoomna, aise kapde pehenna phir risk badh jata hai. Mardon ko toh hum ek din mein nahi badal sakte, isliye practical baat yehi hai ki apna khayal rakho.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their personal responsibility and normalizes male predatory behavior as inevitable. It encodes protective/practical advice, restriction on movement and dress, and the 'we can't change men' inevitability framing.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. You are at a kitty party with seven women from your neighbourhood. One woman mentions that her niece who is 29 just got engaged. Everyone congratulates her and someone says thank god, it was getting late. The conversation turns to late marriages in general and what happens to women who wait too long and what the right age is and what parents are doing wrong when their daughters reach 28 and 29 still unmarried. You are participating in this conversation and giving your opinion on what the correct window for a woman's marriage is and what closes after that window shuts.
